system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

adjustTimeStep  yes;

maxCo           0.5;

maxDeltaT       0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss upwind;
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi)        Gauss upwind;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //')]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis>## Error Diagnosis

The error is:

```
attempt to read beyond EOF
file: .../system/fvSchemes/divSchemes/div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) at line 30.
```

This has persisted across 7 attempts. Every time, the solver cannot parse the divergence scheme entry `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))` in `divSchemes`. The entry exists in the file but the parser still fails at line 30.

## Root Cause

After carefully examining the provided `fvSchemes` file, I notice the `divSchemes` section has **extra blank lines and comments** that may be confusing the parser. More importantly, looking at the exact current file content, the `divSchemes` section ends at line 29, and line 30 is the opening brace of the `laplacianSchemes` section. The error says "at line 30" which refers to the `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))` entry that should be in `divSchemes` but apparently isn't being parsed correctly despite being present.

The real issue across all attempts is that the **format of the divergence scheme entry** matters. In OpenFOAM 10, the entry `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;` with the complex nested parentheses in the field name cannot be parsed when it spans multiple lines or has formatting issues.

## New Solution — Different Approach

Instead of trying to parse the complex divergence scheme entry directly, **define the deviatoric stress divergence using `laplacianSchemes` with a `tensorSymm` format**, which is how OpenFOAM 10's pitzDaily tutorial actually handles it. The key change:

### 1. Remove `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))` from `divSchemes` entirely

Delete the line:
```
div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;
```

from `divSchemes`.

### 2. Add it to `laplacianSchemes` with the correct syntax

In the `laplacianSchemes` sub-dictionary, add:
```
div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;
```

This is the standard approach — the stress divergence term is a second-order tensor divergence and belongs in `laplacianSchemes`, not `divSchemes`. The previous attempts put it in `divSchemes` thinking that's where it must go, but it never worked. Moving it to `laplacianSchemes` is the correct placement.

### 3. Also clean up `gradSchemes` — ensure `grad(p)` is properly defined

Verify `grad(p)` is set to `Gauss linear;` (it already is in the current file, so this is fine).

### Summary

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `system/fvSchemes` | Remove `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;` from `divSchemes` |
| `system/fvSchemes` | Add `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind;` to `laplacianSchemes` |

This approach moves the divergence scheme to the correct sub-dictionary (`laplacianSchemes`) where OpenFOAM 10's `pimpleFoam` expects to find complex tensor divergence terms like the deviatoric stress. All previous attempts placed it in `divSchemes` where it consistently failed. The `laplacianSchemes` section is specifically designed for handling the `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))` operator used in the momentum equation's viscous stress computation.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/fvSchemes', 'changes': 'Remove div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind; from divSchemes sub-dictionary; Add div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss upwind; to laplacianSchemes sub-dictionary'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-04.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
